reasoning: The forecast horizon is extremely short (under two months from the May 2025 knowledge cutoff to July 1, 2025). An amphibious invasion of Taiwan by the PRC would require a massive, unprecedented mobilization of troops, materiel, and logistics that would be visible to US intelligence and commercial satellites many months in advance. While China conducted 'Strait Thunder-2025A' military exercises around Taiwan in early April 2025, these were limited to a few days and concluded without an invasion force massing. US intelligence consistently points to 2027 as the earliest target date Xi Jinping has given his military to be capable of an invasion, and there are no official US government warnings of an imminent attack as of early May 2025. Due to the lack of logistical buildup and the very brief remaining timeframe, the probability of an invasion occurring before July 2025 is effectively zero. Therefore, I cap my estimate at the minimum allowed probability of 2%.
